subject: Computer Networking
title: Top-of-Rack Switches
description: Learn Top-of-Rack Switches in one minute.

hook.text: In a data center, every server rack has its own switch at the top. It's called a ToR switch.

buildup.text: Servers connect to the ToR with short copper cables. The ToR uplinks to the fabric with fiber.

discovery.text: ToR switches keep most traffic local. Server-to-server within a rack never leaves the switch.

twist.text: White-box switches run open-source OS like SONiC. Google and Facebook build their own, skipping Cisco.

climax.text: A single ToR handles 25-100 Gbps per server port and 400 Gbps uplinks. Enormous throughput.

punchline.text: The ToR switch is every server's first hop. Speed starts here.

quiz.question: Why are top-of-rack switches placed in each server rack?
options: To keep local server traffic within the rack and reduce latency
To provide wireless access to the data center
To convert AC power to DC for the servers
